summary: Playin‑Wheel jam shines, tight jams & strong The Other One
blurb: The show delivers a spectacular Playin‑Wheel‑Playin sandwich and a fire‑ball second set anchored by The Other One and The Music Never Stopped. Jerry’s guitar is on point, Phil’s bass solos lock the groove, and Keith adds sweet keyboard textures throughout.
review: July 14, 1976 at San Francisco’s Orpheum Theatre captures one of those classic mid‑’70s Dead nights that still feels fresh. The first set builds nicely into the legendary Playin‑Wheel‑Playin sandwich – a soaring, fluid jam where Jerry “busts out the wawa” and Keith weaves sweet chords under the groove. The second set kicks off with Brown Eyed Women and Samson & Delilah before diving into a tight run of Friend Of The Devil, Let It Grow (with a brief vocal slip that sparked some good‑natured chuckling), and a gorgeous cascade of Eyes Of The World → Wharf Rat → The Other One that showcases Phil’s bass solo brilliance and the band’s effortless transitions. Highlights like Looks Like Rain and Scarlet Begonias add color, while the encore Johnny B. Goode leaves the crowd buzzing. Across all six recordings the performance is consistently praised; the best‑sounding soundboard (gd76-07-14.sbd.vernon.18594.sbeok.shnf) captures the magic with clear mix and just enough ambience to feel like you’re in the house.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: All six recordings are positive or mixed, with five explicitly praising the performance (e.g., “magical,” “fluid Garcia,” “excellent soundboard”). No consistent negative comments appear; the strong, repeated acclaim for Playin‑Wheel, The Other One and band cohesion justifies a high positive adjustment.

best_recording.reason: Excellent soundboard transfer with clear mix, captures the magical Playin‑Wheel jam and overall band tightness without major dropouts.

key_highlights: Spectacular Playin‑Wheel‑Playin sandwich that defines the first set
Phil’s bass solo in the second‑set flow and seamless segue into The Other One
Keith’s sweet keyboard work on Eyes Of The World and Friend Of The Devil
High‑energy second set with strong transitions and audience‑patched finale

band_performance.Jerry: Fluid, soaring lead guitar throughout; praised for perfect tone and ‘busting out the wawa’ during the Playin‑Wheel jam, with only a slight helium‑like vocal quirk on one tape.
band_performance.Phil: Delivers a standout bass solo in the second set and leads a smooth segue into Music, anchoring the band’s groove.
band_performance.Bob: Minor vocal slip on Let It Grow noted, but overall solid rhythm guitar; his chuckle at the lyric mistake adds charm.
band_performance.Keith: Sweet, pleasurable keyboard textures, especially on Eyes Of The World and Friend Of The Devil; described as ‘on the ball’ and a key part of the jam flow.
